# Signal-Options Trading System — Full Review Synthesis

**Review basis:** working tree on top of HEAD 285dedf4 (tsc clean, signal-options suites 29/29). **Important working-tree note:** a concurrent session fixed the two top verified findings *during this review* — the wire-trail ENFORCE coupling and the deltaSizedGiveback ×100 bug are now resolved in uncommitted code with a new test file (`signal-options-wire-trail-enforce.test.ts`, 4 tests). I re-verified this directly (exit-policy.ts:431 `usesWireTrail = wireTrailEligible && input.wireTrailEnforceEnabled === true`; ×100 removed with an explanatory comment; enforce flag wired at automation.ts:12329 and 16798). Line numbers cited below may drift ±10 in `signal-options-exit-policy.ts` due to that diff.

---

## 1. SYSTEM MAP

### Exit policy engine (stop / trail / greek wire trail)
- **Hard stop:** `entry × (1 + hardStopPct/100)`, hardStopPct clamped to [-100, 0]. Defaults: **-40% base, -30% aggressive/tuned**. Trigger: mark ≤ stop while no trail has taken over → `hard_stop`. Exit fills use a bid-weighted mid (mid − 0.9×(mid−bid)), so a gap through the stop realizes slippage, not the stop level.
- **Legacy trail:** activates at peak return ≥ trailActivationPct (**40 base / 35 aggressive**); stop = max(entry×(1+minLockedGainPct/100), peak×(1−givebackPct/100)) → `runner_trail_stop`. **Progressive ratchet** (aggressive only): 5 steps from 20% peak (giveback 30/lock 0) to 100% peak (giveback 15/lock 60); peak is monotonic so the stop only tightens.
- **Wire/greek trail (default OFF both profiles):** baseline rung by peak profit (35%→wire3, 65%→wire2, 100%→wire1, 200%→trendLine); greek adjustment shifts the rung ±1 — tighten on delta decay ≤ −0.1, theta burden ≥ 8%/day of premium, or spread widening; loosen only if no tighten fired and delta improved ≥0.05 with gamma ≥0.05. `wire_structure_break` fires on a completed underlying bar crossing the selected wire (direction-aware for puts via bearWires), suppressed on the exact regime-flip bar. **As of this working tree, the wire trail is telemetry-only unless `PYRUS_SIGNAL_OPTIONS_WIRE_TRAIL_ENFORCE=1`** — enforce-off now leaves stop behavior byte-for-byte legacy (telemetry reports `active` vs `enforced` separately). Delta-sized giveback (default off) is now correctly per-share: |delta|×|spot−wire|.
- **Greek freshness:** greeks must be ≤ greekMaxAgeMs (**default 15s**, clamp 1s–300s); with requireFreshGreeks (default true) stale greeks zero the rung adjustment and delta sizing but do **not** disarm the structure break (degrades to baseline rung). Worker poll accelerates to ≤20s when the wire trail is on.
- **Early invalidation:** only while no trail active; base profile disabled (0/0), aggressive 8 bars / −25% (quality-conditioned 4/−15% low, 8/−25% high when conditional exits on, default off).
- **Overnight:** 15:45–16:00 ET window only; `overnight_risk_exit` when mark return < overnightMinGainPct (base 20 but disabled; aggressive 10 enabled; high-quality bullish gets −100 = always hold), else `overnight_runner_stop` at max(lock floor, peak×(1−overnightRunnerGivebackPct)). Uses legacy activation only, ignores progressive/wire by design.

### Entry path (greek-based contract selection)
- Selector is gated by policy + runtime mode; **tuned profile enables it** (mode 'all', minScore 0, maxCandidates 24, requireLiveGreeks true, fallbackToLegacy true); base default is off. Universe: **one expiration** (DTE min/target/max = 1/1/3, no 0DTE) and **~7 strikes** around the money — the maxCandidates=24 slice never binds.
- Scoring (100 pts): deltaFit 30 (peak |delta| 0.45), breakevenFit 20 (vs 1σ expected move), gammaTheta 15, ivValue 15 minus penalties, liquidity 10, dataQuality 10. Hard disqualifier: |delta| < 0.15 (the DIA 471P fix). Post-selection guards: stale-chain reject + 6-median-strike-step moneyness guard.
- With requireLiveGreeks=true, candidates are batch-hydrated via IBKR live demand within a 2.5s settle budget; the winner is re-hydrated and its order plan rebuilt from the live quote (liquidity/premium re-enforced; **score/delta/breakeven are not re-checked** — see findings). Greek failure routes to the legacy slot picker (`fallback_legacy`) which applies only the liquidity gate.
- Entry gating order: MTF alignment (as-of signal time, put blocklist for inverse ETFs) → gateway/daily-loss/open-symbol blockers → contract resolution → session gate → trading-allowance sizing. No macro/earnings event gate exists.

### Exit execution, failsafes, ledger reconcile
- In-scan exits are session-gated; shadow deployments fall back to delayed/mark mid (rejected below $0.01). Duplicate-exit protection is an in-process claim map (10-min TTL) — **used only on the quote/tick exit path**, not the opposite-signal flip close (finding #3). Exit events mirror into the shadow ledger idempotently (sourceEventId + clientOrderId dedup; over-sell guard 409s duplicates so cash stays correct).
- Failsafe stack: (1) mark-time stop enforcement during shadow mark refresh; (2) per-tick maintenance sweep — expiration close, force-close on re-derived stop breach or −90% drawdown floor, price ladder mark→intrinsic→$0.01; (3) `ledger_reconcile` heals closed-without-exit drift (30-day lookback). The force-close pass has a deployment+symbol-since-openedAt exit-event dedup guard; the mark-time and expiration emitters **do not** (finding #4). All failsafes are shadow-only; live-broker routing has none (currently moot — automation is shadow-only by design).

### Live mark path (worker + tick manager)
- Worker: 5s wakeup; per-deployment poll 15–3600s (default 60s), tightened to 5s with open positions; cockpit push events force immediate scans; advisory-lock + 120s timeout guard. **Uncommitted:** resource pressure now degrades to `skipEntryWork` (positions still get marks/exits) instead of pausing the deployment.
- Tick manager: per-position push subscriptions via the **new unified `option-quote-demand-coordinator`** (old coordinator is now a re-export shim — scan path and tick path share one registry). Latest-tick-wins buffering, serialized manageQuote, greek-demand flips carry the buffered tick across resubscribe. Upstream cadence is a ~2s snapshot poll, so ticks are ~0.5 Hz/position.
- Stops cannot fire on stale data: exits require live-eligible quotes or a shadow-mark fallback ≤60s old from a live session; marks tolerate 3 min. Peak price is floored by a DB shadow-mark read on every call — currently unthrottled (finding #9).

### In-flight push-native fold ("running tally", uncommitted)
Steps 1–3 done (fold module + 12-test equivalence suite; watermark tail-read with 5-min overlap; per-deployment projection map). Step 4 done via per-tick shadow-ledger reconcile, **but the planned mismatch→drift-metric→rebuild path was never built in any mode** (finding #8). Step 5–6 code done: `SIGNAL_OPTIONS_TALLY` off/shadow/on with four dual-run drift compares in shadow mode and full authority flip + firehose-skip write-cut in on mode. **The flag is set nowhere — the fold does not execute at all today.** Bake not started; drift counters are test-export-only with no diagnostics surface.

### Config surface
Backend schema is the source of truth with a tolerant clamping resolver. Backend default profile is conservative (−40 stop, $500/entry, all trails off); the tuned patch (applied per-deployment via script) is the aggressive set. The **UI default profile equals the tuned patch**, so UI and backend defaults disagree on every major exit knob — blast radius is mainly backtests (which fall through to the conservative default) since seeded deployments persist the tuned profile. Six `greekSelector` knobs, `mtfPattern`, `allowanceBasis`, advanced wire thresholds, and the put blocklist have no UI surface; the forced-on normalization for `mtfAlignment.enabled`/`requireBidAsk`/`requireFreshQuote` runs only on the UI fallback path, not the primary server-resolved path.

---

## 2. RANKED FINDINGS

### Resolved during this review cycle (verify at commit time, keep the tests)
- ✅ **Wire trail changed real exits with ENFORCE off** (was high/CONFIRMED) — fixed: `usesWireTrail` now requires the enforce flag; enforce-off is byte-for-byte legacy; 4 new tests pin it. Uncommitted.
- ✅ **deltaSizedGiveback ×100 unit bug** (was medium/CONFIRMED) — fixed: per-share formula, telemetry-only unless enforced. Uncommitted.

### Open — verified (CONFIRMED), ranked by real-money risk

**1. Contract change on an open position permanently kills its push-tick subscription** — HIGH, bug, CONFIRMED (re-verified still open: `releaseOptionQuoteDemand` at option-quote-demand-coordinator.ts:373-382 is still purely owner-keyed).
Runtime keys include providerContractId but the demand owner doesn't; on contract change, reconcile installs the new demand then the stale-key sweep's owner-keyed release destroys the *just-created* demand, and the zombie runtime prevents any resubscribe. Push marks (the sub-second stop reaction path) die silently until restart; the worker's 5s scan (separate owner) keeps marks alive at scan cadence only, with zero diagnostics.
*Evidence:* tick-manager.ts:116,123,293-333; coordinator.ts:325-332,365-382. *Fix direction:* generation-token or demand-identity check on release; add a contract-change test (current suite pins one CONTRACT_ID only).

**2. Legacy fallback silently drops every greek safety check when live greeks are unavailable** — MEDIUM, risk, CONFIRMED.
With requireLiveGreeks=true (tuned), a failed 2.5s hydration rejects all candidates and falls back to the slot picker with only the liquidity gate — the 0.15-delta disqualifier and all scoring vanish exactly when the greek feed is degraded. Stale-chain + moneyness guards remain, but a ≤6-step strike on wide-step/short-DTE chains can still carry |delta| < 0.15. Only diagnostics distinguish it (`fallback_legacy`).
*Evidence:* automation.ts:3720-3728,3783-3813,3394-3513. *Fix direction:* enforce a minimum-|delta| floor (or moneyness proxy) in the fallback path; consider skipping entry instead when greek data is down for N consecutive attempts.

**3. Opposite-signal flip close bypasses the exit-claim dedup guard** — MEDIUM, bug, CONFIRMED (re-verified: only claim call site is automation.ts:12470).
`closePositionForOppositeSignal` emits an exit event with no claim and against a snapshot taken before entry work; a concurrent tick-manager stop exit produces two exit events for one position. Ledger cash survives (over-sell guard) but `computeSignalOptionsDailyRealizedPnl` has no per-position dedup, so the daily-loss halt double-counts — the exact failure the claim map documents.
*Evidence:* automation.ts:14184-14232 (no claim), 11916-11941 (guard rationale), 7139-7154. *Fix direction:* route the flip close through the same claim + a fresh position re-check.

**4. Mark-time stop enforcement and expiration ledger-sync lack the exit-event dedup the force-close has** — MEDIUM, bug, CONFIRMED.
If an in-scan exit's mirror sell fails (warn-swallowed), the shadow row stays open; the next mark refresh or expiry sweep emits a second pnl-bearing exit event, double-counting into the daily halt. The mark-path duplicate needs a subsequent stop breach; the expiration duplicate is unconditional for an orphaned-open expired row.
*Evidence:* shadow-account.ts:4857-4864,5294-5344 (no guard) vs 5425-5439 (force-close guard); automation.ts:2256-2264. *Fix direction:* reuse the deployment+symbol-since-openedAt existence check (or better, position-scoped) in both emitters.

**5. gammaTheta score component is dimensionally inconsistent (dollars ÷ premium-fraction)** — MEDIUM, bug, CONFIRMED (re-verified still present at option-greek-selector.ts:342-348).
`gamma×spot²×1e-4` (dollars, and 2× the true 1%-move gamma P&L) divided by |theta|/entryPrice (dimensionless) leaves a spot-scaled ratio: on ~$600 underlyings the component saturates at 15 pts for every candidate; on ~$20 underlyings identical gamma/theta efficiency scores a fraction. Selection discriminates by underlying price level, and a fixed minScore is easier/harder to clear cross-symbol purely by price.
*Fix direction:* divide gammaMoveValue by entryPrice (premium-fraction gained per 1% move vs premium-fraction lost per day); pin with component-level tests.

**6. Greek score computed on the snapshot quote; entry fills at a later live re-quote with no re-validation** — MEDIUM, risk, CONFIRMED.
The settle loop (≤2.5s, may serve the demand cache) replaces the quote and rebuilds the order plan; liquidity/premium are re-enforced but minScore, the 0.15-delta floor, and breakeven-vs-expected-move are never re-checked at the final price/greeks. The shadow delayed/mark fallback bypasses even the fresh-quote requirement without re-scoring.
*Evidence:* automation.ts:3730-3737,13169-13277,13286-13333. *Fix direction:* cheap re-score (or at minimum delta-floor + breakeven re-check) on the final quote, reject/downgrade on failure.

**7. Every pushed tick runs an unthrottled shadow-mark fallback DB read per position** — MEDIUM, risk, CONFIRMED (as corrected).
`refreshActivePosition` unconditionally awaits `readShadowPositionMarkFallback` (2 SELECTs, one scanning up to 1000 rows) every tick. Tick cadence is capped at ~0.5 Hz/position by the 2s snapshot poll (not unbounded as originally claimed), and snapshot *writes* are pressure-gated — but the fallback *read* is ~2N queries/2s continuously on the same pool the new `skipEntryWork` change protects.
*Fix direction:* short-TTL in-memory cache of the peak floor per position (peak is monotonic; invalidate on exit), or read only every Nth tick.

**8. Fold "on" mode has zero drift detection and never self-repairs; plan step 4's rebuild path unbuilt in any mode** — MEDIUM, risk, CONFIRMED. *(Blocks the tally flip.)*
All four drift compares run only in shadow mode; reconcile filters positions but records no metric and never invalidates the projection. An event landing >5 min behind the watermark (the reconcile healer writes `occurredAt = closedAt`, which routinely exceeds the overlap) is never folded: positions are saved by per-tick reconcile, but dedup/dailyPnl/control carry the drift until restart. (Absorbs the low finding "5-minute overlap permanently misses backdated reconcile exits".)
*Evidence:* automation.ts:7455-7515, compares at 18662/18691/18775/18818, overlap 170/6968-6986; shadow-account.ts:5669-5698. *Fix direction:* on-mode reconcile-mismatch counter + projection invalidation; fold backdated events by createdAt or force-rebuild when a reconcile heal lands.

**9. Tally=on makes skip history memory-only on a VM that rotates ~6h** — MEDIUM, risk, CONFIRMED (as corrected). *(Owner-accepted for firehose skips; misclassification part is not.)*
Under `SIGNAL_OPTIONS_TALLY=on`, every entry-candidate skip is memory-only (2,500 cap, lost each rotation) — including `position_exit_quote_unavailable` (the breached-stop-with-no-quote audit trail) and `after_hours_option_exit_blocked`, which are position-management diagnostics, not firehose. Correction from verification: the dedup-reset consequence does *not* apply to those two reasons (no signalKey / force-retryable); restart dedup loss is real only for terminal signalKey-bearing entry skips.
*Fix direction:* reclassify `position_exit_quote_unavailable` (and arguably the after-hours exit marker) as position-mark skips so they persist; restate the accepted firehose tradeoff in the bake checklist.

**10. Reconcile overwrites in-memory position state with a snapshot up to ~20s stale; dropped spike ticks can lose trailing-peak highs** — MEDIUM, risk, CONFIRMED.
5s reconcile replaces the fresher manageQuote-updated position with a 15s-TTL cached snapshot; combined with single-slot latest-wins tick buffering, a transient high can fail to anchor the trail (probabilistic — the DB peak floor and a background sampler catch most). Bounded-loosening risk only, never a wrong fire.
*Fix direction:* keep max(peakPrice) when merging snapshot over runtime position; or skip overwrite when runtime position is newer.

**11. Stranded-gate guard: forced `mtfAlignment.enabled`/`requireBidAsk`/`requireFreshQuote` only run on the UI fallback path** — MEDIUM, bug, CONFIRMED.
The primary draft path seeds from the server-resolved profile, which preserves a stored `false`; these fields have no rendered control, so a legacy/API-set `false` silently disables MTF confluence for real-money entries while the SIGNAL FRAMES band renders as if it governs gating, and every save round-trips the hidden false. Whether any production row stores false is unverified (one DB query would settle it).
*Fix direction:* move the forced-on normalization server-side into the resolver (or a migration), and/or surface the toggles.

**12. UI default profile and backend default profile disagree on every major exit/risk knob** — MEDIUM, risk, CONFIRMED (as corrected).
UI default = tuned patch (−30/$1500/trails ON); backend default = conservative (−40/$500/trails OFF). Narrowed blast radius: seeded deployments persist the tuned profile so live/shadow stops match the UI; the conservative set actually governs backtests built from strategy parameters and any legacy config lacking a signalOptions block — so backtest results systematically diverge from what the UI displays as the same strategy's exits.
*Fix direction:* one exported default (UI imports or fetches the server default; tuned stays an explicit patch); make backtests resolve the deployment's actual profile.

### Open — unverified (merged/deduped), highest first

**13. Exit-policy math is effectively untested on its positive paths** — HIGH, incomplete. *(Merges 5 findings: trailing-ratchet, wire positive path, stale-greeks handling, overnight exit, early invalidation.)* The new enforce test file covers enforce on/off + delta sizing only. Still zero tests for: progressive-step selection, the max(lock floor, giveback) trail formula and takeover crossover, rung-by-profit baseline, greek tighten/loosen and `selectUsableWireValue` fallback, the short-side structure break (`latestClose >= wire` — no test at all), regime-flip suppression, `resolveGreekFreshness` branches (stale greeks silently disabling tightening), and the entire overnight function (`overnight_risk_exit`/`overnight_runner_stop`). A max→min inversion or step mis-selection would pass the whole suite while loosening real-money stops. Evidence: rg over `*.test.ts` finds none of these symbols/reasons.

**14. Failsafe and restart integration paths are asserted in comments, not tests** — HIGH, incomplete. *(Merges: force-stop/expiry maintenance untested, double-sell after restart/claim-expiry, gap-through-stop orchestration, reconcile-semantics plumbing-only tests.)* Untested load-bearing guards: backfill exclusion in force-close (a bug there force-sells research positions into the live ledger), force-close daily-halt dedup, the restart window where an exit event exists but the mirror order isn't reconciled (could re-derive the position open and sell twice), and the quote-unavailable fallback exit fill/provenance path. Evidence: no test references detectShadowMaintenanceBreach/runShadowMaintenance/the clientOrderId patterns; automation.test.ts:958-963's own comment defers to an unconstructed scenario.

**15. Greek selector's candidate universe (~7 strikes, one expiration) makes maxCandidates=24 illusory; pipeline has no integration tests** — MEDIUM, incomplete. *(Merges 2 findings.)* The chain fetch is sized for the legacy slot picker, so the scorer acts as a near-ATM tiebreaker and can't reach its 0.45-delta peak on short-DTE chains; no test exercises selection, fallback routing, minScore rejection, mode gating, or hydration merge (internals test hook exists at automation.ts:19253).

**16. Tally rollout tail: nothing live, bake unobservable** — MEDIUM, incomplete. *(Merges: flag-off/uncommitted status; drift counters test-export-only.)* `SIGNAL_OPTIONS_TALLY` is set nowhere; all tally code is uncommitted; the plan's "hard zero drift across restarts" gate has no diagnostics route/flight-recorder surface and counters reset each 6h rotation, so the bake criterion currently has no durable evidence trail.

**17. Greek/config knobs with no UI surface** — MEDIUM, incomplete. *(Merges: greekSelector 6 knobs invisible + silently dropped from UI-created deployments' baseline; mtfPattern promote endpoint with no UI loop; allowanceBasis unreachable; advanced wire thresholds hardcoded; blockedPutSymbols list not editable; tradingAllowance blank on fallback draft.)* Worst two: UI-created deployments get tuned exits but greekSelector *disabled* (inconsistent with the tuned server profile), and an API-enabled mtfPattern gate constrains all entries invisibly with no way to inspect or disable from the app.

**18. Wire-trail runtime hardening gaps before ENFORCE flip** — LOW-MEDIUM, risk. *(Merges: no wireContext bar-age guard — a Friday-close bar can drive a Monday structure break; greekMaxAgeMs 15s default is tighter than the 15–20s poll floor, so greek adjustments may silently zero every evaluation — confirmable via lastWireTrail.greekAgeMs telemetry; regime-flip bar has no exit reason and mutes the wire for one bar, undocumented; UI/display stop diverges from enforced stop when conditional exits or wire trail are on.)*

**19. Lifecycle/robustness polish (worker + tick manager + coordinator)** — LOW. *(Merges: tick-manager stop() doesn't cancel in-flight reconcile → post-stop subscription leak; a throwing deployment aborts reconcile before the stale-key sweep; worker scans deployments sequentially so one 120s scan delays others' 5s cadence; coordinator same-owner/same-signature dedupe keeps the old callback with a destructive unsubscribe — a footgun for the next consumer; mid-tick `state_refreshed` events dropped, not deferred.)*

**20. Remaining low/polish items** — LOW. Symbol-keyed force-close/reconcile dedup can suppress closing a second same-symbol position (comments acknowledge the approximation); failsafe blind during total quote outage with no "mark too stale to know" telemetry; no stop-failsafe for non-shadow deployments (moot until live routing); entryGreeks not persisted for synthetic-greek selections (baseline inconsistency, latent under tuned profile); restart loses entry-greek baseline → delta_decay tightening silently disabled for recovered positions (unpinned); MTF gate silent-pass shape in legacy filterState fallback; entry-gate-blocked candidates still consume live-greek demand/settle budget for diagnostics; signal-time spot used for scoring/breakeven/moneyness with no live-spot cross-check; put/call tie-break asymmetry on exact score ties; duplicate greek-adjustment computation; dead `underlyingSpot` parameter; mirror-repair permanently counts maintenance events as missing; projection config-signature on `deployment.updatedAt` could silently degrade on-mode to per-tick full rebuilds (grep updateDeployment frequency before flip); self-referential fold golden (bake is the real guarantee); allowance cache deferred-by-decision.

*Dropped:* nothing — no finding was refuted. Two were fixed in-flight (top of section).

---

## 3. FINISH/POLISH PLAN CANDIDATES

Each 0.5–2 days; ordered by real-money risk reduction. (These align with the existing task list T3–T17.)

1. **Exit dedup closure** (findings #3, #4; T13) — Route the flip close through the exit-claim map + fresh position re-check; add the deployment+symbol-since-openedAt guard to the mark-time stop emitter and expiration ledger-sync; add a per-position dedup (or positionId key) to `computeSignalOptionsDailyRealizedPnl`. Tests for each duplicate scenario. **~1 day.**

2. **Tick-manager subscription lifecycle + read throttle** (findings #1, #7, #19; T14) — Generation-token the coordinator release so an owner-keyed unsubscribe can't kill a newer demand; per-deployment try/catch in reconcile; stop() cancels in-flight reconcile; merge snapshot with `max(peakPrice)`; short-TTL cache on the shadow-mark peak-floor read. Contract-change and stop-mid-reconcile tests. **~1.5–2 days.**

3. **Exit-policy positive-path test battery** (finding #13; T9/T10/T11) — Direct tests for progressive steps, trail floor/takeover, rung-by-profit + greek tighten/loosen + wire fallback order, short-side structure break, regime-flip suppression, greek freshness branches, overnight risk/runner precedence, early invalidation tiers. This is the prerequisite for ever flipping ENFORCE. **~2 days.**

4. **Failsafe/restart integration tests** (finding #14; T11/T12) — Construct: force-close dedup + backfill exclusion; restart inside the exit-event-persisted/mirror-unreconciled window (assert no double sell); gap-through-stop with quote-unavailable → fallback fill provenance. **~1.5 days.**

5. **Greek selector correctness pass** (findings #2, #5, #6, #15; T3) — Normalize gammaTheta by entryPrice; re-validate delta floor + breakeven (or full re-score) on the final live quote; enforce a minimum-|delta| floor in the legacy fallback; decide strike-universe width vs maxCandidates honestly; add pipeline tests via the internals hook. **~2 days.**

6. **Tally finish line: drift self-repair + observability + bake** (findings #8, #9, #16; T4/T5/T8/T17) — Reclassify `position_exit_quote_unavailable` as a persisted position-mark skip; add on-mode reconcile-mismatch counter + projection rebuild trigger; handle backdated reconcile-heal events; expose drift stats via a diagnostics route/flight recorder; then commit the tally hunks and start the shadow bake with a written gate-flip checklist. **~2 days.**

7. **Config-surface coherence** (findings #11, #12, #17; T16) — Move forced-on gate normalization into the server resolver (+ one DB query to check for stranded `false` rows); single source of truth for defaults (UI imports/fetches server default; backtests resolve the deployment profile); surface greekSelector state (at least read-only) and an mtfPattern indicator/disable in the UI. **~1.5–2 days.**

8. **Wire-trail pre-flip hardening** (finding #18) — Bar-age guard on wireContext (reject structure breaks off bars older than N sessions/minutes); reconcile greekMaxAgeMs default vs poll cadence (verify via lastWireTrail.greekAgeMs on a live position); document/decide the regime-flip one-bar mute; feed signalQuality/wireContext into the display-stop path so users see the stop that will actually fire. **~1 day.**
